lessons: Value convention: `"sha256:" + hashlib.sha256(proc.stdout).hexdigest()` on RAW stdout bytes (`proc.stdout` is bytes from `capture_output=True`). Identical prefix to `integrity.file_hash`. The harness

Whitelist match is on the EXACT command string == `Measurement.source` (the full `cmd`, NOT the split argv[0]). Whitelist defaults EMPTY → a command_output fact fails closed until host calls `set_comm
Command runs with `cwd=_GROUND_ROOT`, `shell=False`, argv via `shlex.split`. So `set_ground_root(...)` affects where commands run, consistent with file_hash resolution.
Public API now exported from package root: `from plateau import Measurement, set_command_whitelist, command_whitelist, set_ground_root`.
gate()/apply_gate() are kind-agnostic — they only call `grounding.reverify()`, so command_output already flows through the gate with no gate changes needed.
`KNOWN_KINDS` (tuple) is now exported from `plateau.signal` and is the canonical kinds set; `command_output` IS a member, so continuum re-grounds it normally. Unknown kinds now go STALE in `ground()`

emit/inflate are untouched: kind passes as a free-form string via `grounding.kind` ⇄ `grounding_kind`. The guard lives only in `ground()` (which `inflate(fresh=True)` calls), so inflate's stale list n
Module-global state to reset in tests: `set_command_whitelist([])` AND `set_ground_root(...)` per test (both leak across tests).

Value convention (unchanged, harness-matching): `"sha256:" + hashlib.sha256(proc.stdout).hexdigest()` on RAW stdout bytes. Whitelist match = EXACT string == `Measurement.source` (full cmd). Command ru
gate()/apply_gate() are kind-agnostic; no gate changes needed for command_output.
Code is feature-complete; ONLY remaining sub-task is docs (README.md + adapters/claude_code/SKILL.md).

Value convention locked: `"sha256:"+sha256(raw stdout bytes)`, identical prefix to integrity.file_hash. Whitelist match = EXACT full command string == Measurement.source. Fail-closed set: nonzero exit
Public API (already exported from plateau root): Measurement, set_command_whitelist, command_whitelist, set_ground_root. KNOWN_KINDS is in plateau.signal and includes "command_output".
Test-author gotcha for any future test: reset BOTH _CMD_WHITELIST and _GROUND_ROOT in teardown — they are process-global and leak.
open_goals: 1 signal.py: command_output reverify branch (whitelist, sha256 stdout, fail-closed)
4 README.md + adapters/claude_code/SKILL.md: document kind + pending-facts example
stance: implement command_output end-to-end; value='sha256:'+sha256(raw stdout); fail closed on nonzero-exit/non-whitelisted/missing; keep existing 26 tests green
